problem_statement: As AI accelerates code generation, verification and oversight (review, testing, incident triage, compliance) become the bottleneck; the paper surveys developers to identify what AI oversight/support systems should be built and what behavioral constraints keep humans accountable.
magnitude_evidence: 'writing code accounts for roughly one-tenth of their day' yet most AI tooling targets that fraction; survey of 860 Microsoft developers; 'The resulting flow of AI-generated output is already outpacing existing verification practices [40]'
insufficiency_evidence: 'So many of the PR automated comments are just plain wrong' (P609); 'AI is very bad at understanding the context and throwing wrong answers with utmost confidence, makes us waste time instead of helping' (P223); AI output 'already outpacing existing verification practices'
solution_mechanisms: embedded quality gate at authorship time
change-aware test generation and quality gates
pre-merge security advisor with patch suggestions
change risk radar for regression warning
repository context graph for cross-file changes
cross-cutting guardrails (authority scoping, provenance, uncertainty signaling, least-privilege)
key_constructs: four guardrails: authority scoping, provenance, uncertainty signaling, least-privilege
notes: Reports stated preferences (systems developers want but did not have), not adoption or evaluation; single-organization (Microsoft) survey; arXiv preprint.
